resposta:
solution: 
```
class Solution:
    def subtractProductAndSum(self, n: int) -> int:
        digits = [int(i) for i in str(n)]
        product = 1
        sum_digits = 0
        for digit in digits:
            product *= digit
            sum_digits += digit
        return product - sum_digits
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(log n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution efficiently solves the problem by first converting the integer into a list of digits. Then, it calculates the product and sum of the digits in a single pass. The time complexity is O(log n) because the number of digits in the integer is proportional to the logarithm of the integer. The space complexity is also O(log n) because we need to store the digits in a list. The energy implications are low because the solution only involves basic arithmetic operations and does not require any complex computations.
